expected_findings.summary: Form 8889 line 2/6/13 do not reconcile
expected_findings.detail: Form 8889 requires line 2 (contributions), line 6 (allowable), and line 13 (deduction) to reconcile with the TY2025 statutory limit. line 6 is $8,300 but TY2025 family limit is $8,550.

expected_findings.summary: 1 RSU vest lot(s) with basis near $0 — likely double-counted
expected_findings.detail: The W-2 has a Box 12 code V (RSU / NQSO income) and the 1099-B has same-day sale-to-cover lots with a cost basis near zero. RSU vest income is already included in W-2 Box 1 wages; the 1099-B lot cost basis should equal the Box 1 inclusion amount, not zero. Leaving the basis at zero double-counts the vest as both wage income and a capital gain. This is the single most common mechanically detectable error in the public TurboTax record — a $3,200 refund delta is in Intuit's own public record.

expected_findings.summary: Rental loss $7,814 disallowed — AGI $488,900 exceeds phaseout
expected_findings.detail: IRC §469(i) allows up to $25,000 of rental real estate losses against active income for taxpayers who actively participate. The allowance phases out between $100,000 and $150,000 AGI, and is fully disallowed at $150,000+. This return's AGI is $488,900, so the rental loss is fully suspended and carried forward under §469(b).

expected_findings.summary: Consider real-estate-professional status on this rental
expected_findings.detail: The rental is in service for most of the year. If the taxpayer or spouse spent more than 750 hours on real property trades or businesses and materially participated, rental losses become nonpassive and are not subject to §469 limits.

expected_findings.summary: Land not excluded from depreciable basis on Rental City rental
expected_findings.detail: IRS Publication 527 requires residential rental depreciation to be computed on the building value only; land is never depreciable. This property's depreciation record has no land_value / building_value split, so the full purchase price is being depreciated. Using a conservative 25% land allocation, the current year over-deduction is about $2,591. Pull Example County assessor records for the actual split before filing.

expected_findings.summary: Foreign tax $480 can be claimed directly without Form 1116
expected_findings.detail: Total foreign tax paid is $480, under the $600 election threshold. Claim the credit on Schedule 3 without Form 1116 to save filing complexity.

expected_findings.summary: SALT deduction up to $40K MFJ for TY2025 — recover $18,500
expected_findings.detail: The TCJA permanent extension raised the SALT cap from $10,000 to $40,000 MFJ starting in TY2025. State + property tax paid is $28,500, which under the new cap yields a SALT deduction of $28,500 instead of the prior-law $10,000. Verify Schedule A line 5e uses the TY2025 cap.

expected_findings.summary: Check §111 taxable state refund worksheet
expected_findings.detail: A prior year state refund is only taxable to the extent it produced a federal tax benefit (the §111 tax benefit rule). If prior year was at the SALT cap, the refund is usually NOT taxable. Do the worksheet.

expected_findings.summary: Quinn has up to $7,000 of TY2025 IRA headroom
expected_findings.detail: Quinn is 43 years old; the TY2025 IRA contribution limit is $7,000. Deductibility may phase out at high AGI for traditional IRA if covered by an employer plan; Roth IRA phases out at $236K–$246K MFJ.

expected_findings.summary: Reese has up to $7,000 of TY2025 IRA headroom
expected_findings.detail: Reese is 43 years old; the TY2025 IRA contribution limit is $7,000. Deductibility may phase out at high AGI for traditional IRA if covered by an employer plan; Roth IRA phases out at $236K–$246K MFJ.

expected_findings.summary: AGI above Roth IRA phaseout — consider backdoor Roth
expected_findings.detail: AGI $488,900 is above the MFJ Roth IRA phaseout ceiling ($246,000). Direct Roth contributions are not allowed. Consider the backdoor Roth strategy (nondeductible traditional IRA contribution + Roth conversion) if there is no pre-tax IRA balance to trigger pro-rata.

expected_findings.summary: Quinn: $9,500 of 401k/403b headroom
expected_findings.detail: Quinn has contributed $14,000 toward the TY2025 §402(g) limit of $23,500. Another $9,500 can still be deferred before year end.

expected_findings.summary: Reese: $17,500 of 401k/403b headroom
expected_findings.detail: Reese has contributed $6,000 toward the TY2025 §402(g) limit of $23,500. Another $17,500 can still be deferred before year end.

expected_findings.summary: CTC phased to $0 at AGI $488,900
expected_findings.detail: Child Tax Credit phases out $50 per $1,000 of AGI above the phaseout start ($400,000 MFJ).

expected_findings.summary: Large SALT deduction — run Form 6251 to check AMT exposure
expected_findings.detail: Total state + property tax is $25,200. SALT is added back for AMT under §56(b)(1), which can tip a high-income household into AMT even at TY2025 exemption levels.

expected_findings.summary: MACRS depreciation timing differences can create AMT adjustments
expected_findings.detail: Residential rental (27.5-year) and commercial (39-year) MACRS use straight-line which is identical for regular and AMT, so no adjustment — but personal property (5/7-year) uses 200% DB for regular and 150% DB for AMT. Check any §179 / personal-property depreciation for an addback.

expected_findings.summary: Compute AMT foreign tax credit separately on Form 1116
expected_findings.detail: At high income with foreign tax paid, AMT requires a parallel Form 1116 using AMT sourcing rules under §59(a). The regular FTC and AMT FTC are often different.

expected_findings.summary: Underpayment: need $69,300 withheld for 110% safe harbor
expected_findings.detail: Prior year AGI exceeded $150,000, so the §6654 safe harbor requires 110% of prior year total tax ($69,300) to be paid via withholding or quarterly estimates. Currently $58,300 is withheld; consider a Q4 estimated payment or increased withholding of $11,000 to avoid the §6654 penalty.

expected_findings.summary: Need $79,202 withheld to hit 90% current year safe harbor
expected_findings.detail: The alternative safe harbor is 90% of current year tax. This is usually only easier to hit than 110% prior year if income dropped.

expected_findings.summary: Estimated §6654 penalty ~$440
expected_findings.detail: Based on a shortfall of $11,000 and the TY2025 applicable interest rate of ~8%, the underpayment penalty is approximately $440. Consider the annualized income method (Schedule AI) if income was lumpy across quarters.

expected_findings.summary: Use Schedule AI to reduce §6654 penalty on lumpy income
expected_findings.detail: Income arrived unevenly (RSU vest, K-1 distribution, or both). Form 2210 Schedule AI lets you annualize quarterly income so early quarters with little income do not generate a penalty.
